page: The Vultr vx1-m-2c-16g is a virtual machine instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 2 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. It includes 1 GB of network-attached local storage, 1 public IPv4 address, and lacks dedicated GPU hardware. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

Performance benchmarks demonstrate a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core stress-ng CPU performance and single-threaded compression tasks (gzip and bzip3) rank in the top tier (top 10%). However, multi-core stress-ng CPU performance and overall Passmark CPU scores fall into the bottom 25%, reflecting the shared CPU allocation model. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average at 45.11 GB/sec read and 39.66 GB/sec write, while database operations and Redis SET benchmarks (4.06 million operations per second) remain in the lower 25% tier.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio (1:8) paired with shared CPU resources, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require sustained, high-performance multi-core compute. It is well-suited for caching layers, lightweight database replicas, static web serving (which achieves an average throughput of 7.66 GB/sec), and basic text processing or small-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: The Vultr vx1-m-2c-16g is a memory-optimized virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs (2.0 GHz) and 16.0 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 1 GB of network storage and supports AVX-512 and SHA_NI instructions. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression, but weak multi-core and database performance due to its shared CPU allocation. This instance provides a cost-effective option for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads such as caching, static web serving, and lightweight background processing.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-2c-16g is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 1 GB of network storage, delivering strong single-core but limited multi-core performance.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-2c-16g is a memory-optimized server with 2 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 1 GB network storage for low-compute workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server combining high memory density with shared AMD EPYC processors for lightweight, memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per core)
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Optimized for caching, static web serving, and memory-heavy, low-compute tasks
